goal: Make bulk mail cleanup fast — search by sender, then batch-move matching messages to trash or a named folder, behind the existing confirm/undo gate; plus polish recipient autocomplete.
why: This is the milestone's first mail write surface; it must preserve the M2/ADR-003 destructive-action contract — nothing mutates the mailbox without a human confirming the exact scope (count + sample), and every batch move is reversible.
success_criteria: "Move all email from <sender> to trash" produces a confirmable batch that, on confirm, moves every matching message to trash; undo restores them.
Moving matching mail to a named folder works the same way — search → confirm → batch move.
The confirm card shows the batch BEFORE it runs (count + sample subjects), so the operator confirms scope, not a blind action.
Recipient autocomplete suggests known recipients responsively while composing — visible loading + no-match states.
No mailbox write occurs without passing through the DESTRUCTIVE → stagePendingAction → confirm gate, consistent with the M2/ADR-003 contract.

tasks.title: IMAP move adapter + sender-match preview
acceptance_criteria: previewSenderMatches(email, folderHint, from, sampleSize) returns { folderPath, total, sample } where total counts messages whose sender matches from and sample is up to sampleSize newest envelope summaries.

moveMessages(email, sourceFolderHint, uids, destFolderHint) opens the source folder read-write, moves those uids to the resolved destination, and returns { movedCount, destFolderPath, uidMap } from imapflow messageMove CopyResponseObject.uidMap.
Trash routing resolves the \Trash special-use folder via the existing resolveFolder logic.
The seam test mocks imapflow (no socket) and asserts the preview count+sample and that moveMessages calls client.messageMove(uidRange, destPath, { uid: true }) returning the uidMap.
tasks.action: Add SenderMatchPreview/MoveResult interfaces and exported previewSenderMatches + moveMessages to imap.ts, reusing withClient/fetchFolderList/resolveFolder. preview runs client.search({from},{uid:true}) read-only and fetches the last sampleSize envelopes. moveMessages opens the source read-write (omit readOnly:true), calls client.messageMove(uids.join(','),destPath,{uid:true}), maps res.uidMap via Object.fromEntries. Extend imap.test.ts fake client with messageMove + two it() cases.

verification.pattern: export async function moveMessages|export async function previewSenderMatches

tasks.title: Batch move-to-trash / move-to-folder agent tools (confirm-gated, undoable)
acceptance_criteria: Two new TOOL_DEFINITIONS — batch_move_to_trash (mailbox, from, optional folder) and batch_move_to_folder (mailbox, from, destFolder, optional folder) — both added to the DESTRUCTIVE Set.

executeTool's DESTRUCTIVE branch first calls previewSenderMatches to resolve matched UIDs+count+sample, then stages a pending_actions row whose summary shows count + destination + sample subjects and whose args carry mailbox, sourceFolderPath, uids[], destFolderPath.
On confirm, executeConfirmedAction moves the captured UIDs via moveMessages and stores undo_data = { mailbox, destFolderPath, sourceFolderPath, uidMap }.
undoAction moves messages back: moveMessages(mailbox, destFolderPath, Object.values(uidMap), sourceFolderPath); row flips to undone.
REVERSIBLE_TOOLS in BOTH src/app/page.tsx and src/app/finance/page.tsx include the two batch tools so the Undo button renders.
The OpenRouter system-prompt catalogue names the two tools as confirm-staged writes.
A zero-match search stages NO action and returns a readable no-match result to the model.
tasks.action: Add both tool defs after generate_inbox_briefing; add names to DESTRUCTIVE. In executeTool DESTRUCTIVE branch special-case them: call previewSenderMatches; zero matches return no_match JSON without staging; else build destFolderPath, collect uids, compose summary, stagePendingAction args {mailbox,sourceFolderPath,uids,destFolderPath,from}. Add execute + undo cases (import moveMessages). Name tools as staged in client.ts prompt. Add both to REVERSIBLE_TOOLS in page.tsx + finance/page.tsx.

tasks.title: Recipient autocomplete polish
acceptance_criteria: While a debounced fetch for the current token is in flight, the dropdown shows a loading affordance (skeleton or Searching label) instead of staying blank.
When an address-like token returns zero matches, the dropdown shows a single non-selectable 'No matching recipients' row distinct from the closed state.
Selection, Arrow/Enter/Escape keyboard nav, and the reqId stale-response guard continue working unchanged.
New states use existing design tokens only — no new hardcoded colors, no Inter/system-ui.
Loading and empty rows are NOT role=option and aria-activedescendant only ever points at a real suggestion.
tasks.action: Add isLoading set true when a valid looksLikeAddress token starts a debounce window, false on resolve/catch (guarded by myReq stale check). Open when isLoading OR items present OR a completed fetch returned zero for an address-like token. Render a Searching row while loading and a muted aria-disabled 'No matching recipients' row (not role=option) on zero match. Keep onKeyDown/onSelect unchanged. Add .ra-loading/.ra-empty styled-jsx with existing tokens; sonar-sweep behind prefers-reduced-motion.
